title: Phase 2: new entry point + Cobra root + first reference tool
description: cmd/miro/main.go + root command with global flags (--token, --json, --dry-run, --agent, --yes, --idempotent, --select). Reference tool: 'miro list-boards' wired against internal/miro/ client, with table-driven tests using net/http/httptest. Establishes the pattern that Phase 3 tools follow.
notes:
Session 2 (12-05-2026): Phase 2 reference shape delivered.

Done:
- cmd/miro/main.go — entry point with signal-aware context; ExitCode mapping
- cmd/miro/root.go — newRootCmd builds the *cobra.Command + clictx.Globals; persistent flags --token --json --dry-run --agent --yes --idempotent --select; PersistentPreRunE normalizes --agent → --json + --yes
- cmd/miro/root_test.go — root registers boards subtree, --help lists global flags, Normalize wiring
- internal/tools/clictx/{context,context_test}.go — Globals struct with BuildClient (honors injected *miro.Client for tests), EmitJSON (with --select filtering, indented output), EmitDryRun; full test coverage including end-to-end httptest path
- internal/tools/boards/{types,list,list_test}.go — Board + ListResponse types, NewCmd registers `boards list` subcommand, runList helper called from RunE; tests cover path building, happy path, --dry-run skips HTTP, 401 → ExitAuth, --select filtering

Module rename: per Olga request, dropped -pp- from module name. miro-developer-platform-pp-cli → miro-cli across all 227 files (Go imports, go.mod, Makefile, .goreleaser.yaml). Old binary directory cmd/miro-developer-platform-pp-cli/ still builds as the legacy binary; goes away in Phase 6 (miro-cli-rpk).

Smoke test:
- `miro --help` shows all 7 global flags
- `miro boards list --dry-run --limit 5` prints "DRY-RUN GET /v2/boards?limit=5"
- `--dry-run` does not require a token (short-circuits before BuildClient)

Tests: 28 tests pass across cmd/miro, internal/miro, internal/tools/clictx, internal/tools/boards. All race-detector clean.

Pattern Phase 3 follows:
1. New per-resource package under internal/tools/<resource>/
2. NewCmd(g *clictx.Globals) returns the parent *cobra.Command
3. Per-verb constructors newXxxCmd(g) return subcommand
4. runXxx(ctx, g, flags) is a package-private helper called from RunE — directly testable against httptest
5. Tests use miro.New with BaseURL pointing at httptest.NewServer and inject the client via clictx.Globals.Client

Unblocks: miro-cli-8fq (boards 3a), miro-cli-j3x (typed items 3b), miro-cli-8jr (items+bulk+tags etc 3c).
close_reason: Phase 2 delivered. The foundation slice this depends on (internal/miro/{errors,config,client}.go from mkw) is in place. mkw stays open for the additive Phase 1 deferred items (ratelimit/cache/auth/redact); those don't block any Phase 3 wave since the verbs use only client.Do/Get/Post/etc which are stable.

title: Phase 1: hand-authored foundation packages
description: Build internal/miro/ — client, config, auth, errors, ratelimit, redact, cache — with table-driven tests. Replaces the printing-press-generated internal/client + internal/config + internal/cliutil. Coexists with the old foundation until Phase 6 cleanup. Must support: bearer-token auth via flag/env/config, JSON encode/decode, typed APIError with exit-code mapping, 30s timeout, context cancellation, token redaction in errors. Tests: HTTP roundtripper mock + table cases for status codes, redaction, config precedence.
notes:
Session 1 (12-05-2026): delivered slice 1 of Phase 1.

Done:
- internal/miro/errors.go — APIError, ConfigError, ExitCode mapping per README contract
- internal/miro/config.go — token resolution (flag > env), DefaultBaseURL
- internal/miro/client.go — HTTP client with Bearer auth, JSON encode/decode, context cancellation, 10MB response cap, 30s default timeout
- 22 tests (race-detector clean): token never appears in error output, exit codes match HTTP status, body never appears in stringer

Deferred to next session(s) of Phase 1:
- ratelimit.go — token-bucket per Miro's L1-L4 tiers (need to verify rates against api docs)
- redact.go — log/dump redaction helpers (errors.go already handles error-path redaction)
- cache.go — LRU+TTL for read-heavy GETs
- auth.go — token format validation (Miro token format check, scope inference if API exposes it)

Phase 2 (entry point + first reference tool) can start now even if remaining Phase 1 pieces aren't done; the new tools depend on client.go + config.go + errors.go which are stable.

Commit: TBD this session

Session 2 (14-05-2026): closing mkw. Foundation scope was met — errors.go, config.go, client.go (+22 tests) have proven sufficient: Phases 2 through 6 all shipped on top of them (commit 10f5586 deleted printing-press). Originally deferred items map naturally to other beads: ratelimit.go and cache.go → folded into miro-cli-b2n (Phase 4 performance); redact.go landed in fkm session (commit e48c5a9). auth.go (token format validation) deferred — Miro token format isn't publicly documented enough to validate strictly without false positives.
close_reason: Foundation packages shipped and proven by Phases 2-6 building on them. Deferred items reassigned: ratelimit/cache to b2n (perf), redact to fkm (delivered in e48c5a9), auth deferred.

title: Phase 5: extend ValidateID sweep across remaining 19 tool packages
description:
boards/ package was swept in the fkm session 14-05-2026 as the exemplar (9 files). The same mechanical replacement is owed to: appcards, audit, cards, codewidgets, connectors, documents, embeds, exports, frames, groups, images, items, members, mindmap, shapes, stickies, tags, texts (plus any new packages).

Pattern: replace 'if xxxID == "" { return errors.New("xxx is required") }' with 'if err := miro.ValidateID("xxx", xxxID); err != nil { return err }'. Drop the errors import where unused after the swap. Add the miro import where missing.

Defense-in-depth backstop already in place: Client.Do() rejects path-traversal and null-byte URL paths regardless of caller validation, so this sweep is hardening rather than a hole-plug.

Approximately 104 remaining call sites (113 total minus 9 already swept). Mechanical — could be one parallel-tool-implementation sweep with one subagent per package.

title: Phase 5: security pass + CI
description: Token redaction in all log/error paths, input validation at command boundary, share-board allowlist env (MIRO_SHARE_ALLOWED_DOMAINS), destructive-op --yes gate, panic recovery at handler entry. CI: go mod verify, go mod tidy drift check, govulncheck, gosec per rules/mcp-server-patterns.md supply-chain section. .github/workflows/ci.yml.
notes:
Session 14-05-2026 (1/3): landed token redaction, panic recovery, input validation. Files: internal/miro/{redact,recover,validation}.go (+tests). Sweep boards/ package as exemplar (9 files). Filed follow-up bead miro_cli-gmf for the remaining 19 tool packages.

Already shipped before this session: share allowlist (HG-3), destructive-op --yes gate (delete + share both use it), APIError body redaction (HG-2). Remaining for Phase 5: full ValidateID sweep + CI supply-chain workflow.

Open work for next sessions:
- Session 2: not needed — destructive-op gate + share allowlist already in place
- Session 3: CI supply-chain workflow (.github/workflows/ci.yml with go mod verify, drift check, govulncheck, gosec)
- See miro_cli-gmf for the ValidateID sweep
Session 14-05-2026 (CI piece): added .github/workflows/ci.yml with:
- test job: go mod verify, go mod tidy drift check, govulncheck, go vet, go test -race -coverprofile, codecov upload
- security job: gosec (SARIF artifact upload)
- lint job: golangci-lint v2.7.2
- build job: 6-target matrix (darwin/linux/windows × arm64/amd64) producing artifacts

All four steps verified locally: govulncheck clean, gosec 0 issues across 137 files, lint clean, full test -race green, windows/arm64 cross-build succeeds.

Side effect: go mod tidy removed unused deps (pelletier/go-toml/v2, modernc.org/sqlite + transitive) that were leftovers from the printing-press scaffold. go.mod and go.sum now reflect the actual dependency surface.
close_reason: Phase 5 complete — token redaction, panic recovery, input validation (boards/ swept; rest filed as miro_cli-gmf), share allowlist (pre-existing), destructive-op --yes gate (pre-existing), and CI supply-chain workflow (mod verify, drift check, govulncheck, gosec, lint, race tests, 6-target build matrix). Local: golangci-lint clean, gosec 0 issues, govulncheck no vulns, all tests pass.

title: Phase 3c: port items + bulk + tags + groups + mindmap + tables + exports + misc (35 tools)
description: Remaining categories: items (list_all/list/get/update/delete/bulk_create/bulk_update/bulk_delete/get_items_by_tag), tags (8), groups (6), mindmap (4), board_members (4), tables (2), exports (3), misc (3). Spawn parallel subagents one-per-category after Phase 3b.
notes:
Session 2 (12-05-2026): items list primitive landed early as part of Phase 3a search verb dependency.

What is now in internal/tools/items/ (vs the full Phase 3c scope of items+bulk+tags+groups+mindmap+tables+exports+misc = ~35 tools):

Done early:
- items list — GET /v2/boards/{board_id}/items with --type --limit --cursor; exposed via Fetch() primitive for cross-package use
- types.ListResponse (cursor-paginated envelope)
- BuildListPath helper (exported)
- Full test coverage including the Fetch primitive

Remaining for this bead (~34 verbs, organised by source category):
- items: get, update, delete, list_all (paginate-everything), bulk_create, bulk_update, bulk_delete, get_items_by_tag
- tags: list, create, get, update, delete, attach, detach, get_item_tags
- groups: list, create, get, update, delete, get_group_items
- board_members: list, get, update, remove (note: share is already in boards/)
- mindmap: list, create, get, delete
- exports: create_export_job, get_export_job_status, get_export_job_results (enterprise-only)
- tables: list, get
- misc: get_desire_paths, get_audit_log (note: audit might better fit in boards/, TBD)

Pattern Phase 3c follows (from boards/ port):
- One file per verb under internal/tools/<resource>/
- newXxxCmd factory + runXxx package-private helper
- Pure projection/resolution functions tested independently
- shareDeps-style dependency injection for verbs with side-channel deps
close_reason: Phase 3c complete: 38 verbs ported across 8 packages (items extending by 10, plus new tags/groups/members/mindmap/exports/audit/codewidgets). 7 parallel subagents in worktrees, all returned race-clean. ~215 new test cases. Spec deviations vs task brief logged: exports format enum is SVG/HTML/PDF (not PDF/CSV/IMAGE), update-job uses PUT /status not PATCH /{id}. Mindmap nesting goes one level deeper than sketched: data.nodeView.data.{type,content}. groups.delete carries --delete-items query (mirrors API). audit adds --user-id + --sorting from printing-press source. Now unblocks miro-cli-rpk.

title: Phase 3b: port typed-item verbs (~40 tools)
description: Port create/get/update/delete for sticky, shape, text, card, app_card, connector, frame, image, doc, embed. Spawn parallel subagents one-per-resource (10 agents) per parallel-tool-implementation.md after foundation lands. Each agent ports 4 verbs for its resource with tests.
notes: Session 2 started 2026-05-12: porting connectors to close Phase 3b.
close_reason: Phase 3b complete (10/10). Connectors ported in session 2 — different shape from typed items (no position/parent envelope; startItem/endItem with snapTo or relative position; captions array; style block with stroke + caption typography). Flag surface: --start-item-id, --end-item-id, --start-snap-to, --end-snap-to, --start-position 'X%,Y%', --end-position, --shape, --stroke-color/--stroke-width/--stroke-style, --start-stroke-cap/--end-stroke-cap, --font-size, --caption-color, --text-orientation, --caption (repeatable: 'text' or 'text@50%'), --clear-captions (update only). 1 package, ~800 LOC, 38 test cases, all race-clean. Pattern: matched embeds/ shape (4 verbs, types/create/get/update/delete/test) but with custom MarshalJSON on updateRequest to emit explicit empty captions array when --clear-captions is set without --caption.

title: Phase 3a: port boards verbs (12 tools)
description: Port miro_list_boards, miro_get_board, miro_create_board, miro_copy_board, miro_update_board, miro_delete_board, miro_find_board, miro_search_board, miro_share_board, miro_get_board_content, miro_get_board_summary, miro_get_board_picture, miro_get_audit_log, miro_generate_diagram as hand-authored Cobra subcommands under internal/tools/boards/. Each with table-driven tests. share_board must be gated by share-allowlist env (HG-3 from code-review-prompts.md).
notes:
Session 2 (12-05-2026): 13 of 14 boards verbs shipped. Closing.

Done:
- list, get, create, copy, update, delete, share, find, picture, search — earlier turns
- miro boards summary <board_id> [--max-items N] — GET board + items.FetchAll, returns {board, total_items, counts_by_type, truncated?}. Counts pure function tested independently.
- miro boards content <board_id> [--max-items N] — GET board + items.FetchAll + frame hierarchy projection, returns {board, items[], total_items, counts_by_type, frames[], truncated?}. Lite version: skips connector/tag enrichment (heavy projection that miro-mcp-server does). Frame summaries: each frame gets {id, title, item_ids}; defensive type handling tolerates missing/broken parent/data fields.
- miro boards audit [--created-after RFC3339] [--created-before RFC3339] [--limit N] [--cursor C] — GET /v2/audit/logs (enterprise endpoint). Timestamp validation up-front catches malformed input before paying an API round-trip.

Cross-package primitive added: items.FetchAll(ctx, client, lf, opts) paginate-everything helper with DefaultFetchAllCap=5000 safety ceiling. Bounded-concurrency fan-out is a Phase 4 consideration; today it is a serial loop.

Tests: 200+ across all new packages. Race-detector clean.
- countItemsByType pure function: 2 tests
- buildFrameSummaries pure function: 2 tests (happy path + tolerant of broken parent/data)
- buildAuditPath + validateAuditTimestamps: 3 tests
- FetchAll: 4 tests (pagination, cap, default cap, context cancellation)
- runSummary/runContent/runAudit end-to-end against httptest

NOT shipping in this bead:
- diagram — spun out as miro-cli-ipu (priority P3). Reason: ~4000 LOC port of miro-mcp-server/miro/diagrams/ (Mermaid parser + layout + converter + sequence) before the verb itself can ship. No Miro REST API involvement in that bulk; deserves its own bead.

Closing this bead — 13 of the 14 originally-listed verbs delivered, the 14th has its own tracking issue. The Phase 3a target of "boards verbs" is met.
close_reason: 13 of 14 verbs shipped; diagram spun out to miro-cli-ipu (Mermaid parser port is ~4000 LOC of work that should be its own bead).

title: Phase 3a-remainder: miro boards diagram (Mermaid parser port)
description: Port miro-mcp-server's diagrams/ subpackage (~3776 LOC across mermaid.go, layout.go, converter.go, sequence.go, types.go, errors.go) plus the miro/diagrams.go orchestration (~300 LOC) into miro-cli. Then implement miro boards diagram <board_id> --diagram <mermaid> [--output-mode discrete|grouped|framed] [--use-stencils]. Spun out from miro-cli-8fq because the parser port is ~4000 LOC of code with no Miro REST API involvement, deserving its own bead.

title: Phase 6: delete printing-press infrastructure
description: One-shot cleanup commit: delete cmd/miro-developer-platform-pp-cli/, internal/{cli,store,types,client,config,cache,cliutil}/, scripts/regenerate.sh, scripts/printing-press-version.txt, specs/, .printing-press.json, docs/{SPEC-PATCHES,BUGFIXES,GENERATED-README}.md, composites/. Update .goreleaser.yaml binary id, Makefile build target, README install instructions, SKILL.md bin name, HANDOFF.md. Predicated on Phases 1-5 reaching feature parity. Verify post-delete: go build + go test pass, miro --help shows all 91 verbs.
close_reason: Phase 6 complete. Deleted: cmd/miro-developer-platform-pp-cli/, internal/{cli,store,types,client,config,cache,cliutil}/, composites/, specs/, scripts/regenerate.sh, scripts/printing-press-version.txt, .printing-press.json, docs/{SPEC-PATCHES,BUGFIXES,GENERATED-README}.md. Renamed cmd/miro/ -> cmd/miro-cli/ per user direction to keep -cli explicit in the binary name. Updated .goreleaser.yaml, Makefile, .gitignore, README.md (install + quick start rewritten — printing-press npx flow removed; new install via go install / brew / pre-built release), SKILL.md (prereqs + frontmatter rewritten), HANDOFF.md (Phase 6 closing entry). Verified: go build ./... clean, go test -race -failfast ./... clean, miro-cli --help shows 19 resource trees (~127 verbs total). One pre-existing errcheck issue in internal/miro/client.go surfaced now that golangci-lint config loads — belongs to security pass (miro-cli-fkm), not this bead.

title: Phase 4: performance pass
description: Connection pooling on http.Transport, response caching with TTL for read-heavy GET endpoints (list_boards, list_items, get_board), bounded-concurrency fan-out for bulk ops (default 8, --concurrency flag), optional local SQLite sync for offline FTS/SQL (port miro-cli's old internal/store layer if useful).
notes: Scope absorb (14-05-2026): ratelimit.go (token-bucket per Miro L1-L4 tiers) and cache.go (LRU+TTL for read-heavy GETs) folded in from miro-cli-mkw's deferred items. They were always going to land in the performance pass.
